original_question:
On a board, "COMPUTATION" is spelled out with 11 tiles, one letter per tile. Three vowels and four consonants fall off and are put away in a bag. If the Ts are indistinguishable, how many distinct possible collections of letters could be put in the bag?

Present the answer in LaTex format: \boxed{Your answer}

similar_questions.question: On a board, "MATHEMATICS" is spelled out with 12 tiles, one letter per tile. Four vowels and three consonants fall off and are put away in a bag. If the Ms are indistinguishable, how many distinct possible collections of letters could be put in the bag?

On a board, "COMPUTERS" is spelled out with 9 tiles, one letter per tile. Two vowels and five consonants fall off and are put away in a bag. If the Rs are indistinguishable, how many distinct possible collections of letters could be put in the bag?

On a board, "ENGINEERING" is spelled out with 12 tiles, one letter per tile. Three vowels and four consonants fall off and are put away in a bag. If the Ns are indistinguishable, how many distinct possible collections of letters could be put in the bag?

On a board, "DEVELOPMENT" is spelled out with 12 tiles, one letter per tile. Five vowels and two consonants fall off and are put away in a bag. If the Es are indistinguishable, how many distinct possible collections of letters could be put in the bag?

On a board, "INFORMATION" is spelled out with 11 tiles, one letter per tile. Four vowels and three consonants fall off and are put away in a bag. If the Is are indistinguishable, how many distinct possible collections of letters could be put in the bag?
